mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, higher borrowing costs constrain corporate investment and consumer credit demand, reducing expected cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5% and GDPC1 growth decelerates, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with payroll growth > 100k are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real household purchasing power and triggers anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite sustained CPI acceleration above 0.3% monthly, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in personal savings and employment levels indicate robust household balance sheet strength, which sustains consumption-driven corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial output growth signals effective supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative when industrial production accelerates above 0.5% monthly, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for oil price shocks.
